Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. A new C++ back end for ocamlc 220 upvotes, 19 comments
  2. Fast and Gorgeous Erosion Filter 212 upvotes, 21 comments
  3. Lemonade by AMD: a fast and open source local LLM server using GPU and NPU 198 upvotes, 54 comments
  4. Email obfuscation: What works in 2026? 251 upvotes, 75 comments
  5. Quantum computing bombshells that are not April Fools 231 upvotes, 72 comments
  6. Mercor says it was hit by cyberattack tied to compromise LiteLLM 105 upvotes, 31 comments
  7. Inside Nepal's Fake Rescue Racket 124 upvotes, 38 comments
  8. Emacs-libgterm: Terminal emulator for Emacs using libghostty-vt 43 upvotes, 12 comments
  9. Steam on Linux Use Skyrocketed Above 5% in March 590 upvotes, 273 comments
  10. LinkedIn Is Illegally Searching Your Computer 670 upvotes, 327 comments
  11. Built a cheap DIY fan controller because my motherboard never had working PWM 57 upvotes, 19 comments
  12. Sweden goes back to basics, swapping screens for books in the classroom 407 upvotes, 211 comments
  13. New laws to make it easier to cancel subscriptions and get refunds 124 upvotes, 56 comments
  14. 'Backrooms' and the Rise of the Institutional Gothic 27 upvotes, 8 comments
  15. Qwen3.6-Plus: Towards Real World Agents 72 upvotes, 31 comments
  16. Enabling Codex to Analyze Two Decades of Hacker News Data 43 upvotes, 16 comments
  17. The SpaceX IPO: retail investor notes 30 upvotes, 10 comments
  18. Bringing Clojure programming to Enterprise (2021) 128 upvotes, 64 comments
  19. Gone (Almost) Phishin' 108 upvotes, 53 comments
  20. Significant Raise of Reports 133 upvotes, 68 comments
  21. IBM Announces Strategic Collaboration with Arm 194 upvotes, 120 comments
  22. Subscription bombing and how to mitigate it 240 upvotes, 154 comments
  23. EmDash – A spiritual successor to WordPress that solves plugin security 635 upvotes, 474 comments
  24. Artemis computer running two instances of MS outlook; they can't figure out why 14 upvotes, 5 comments
  25. Artemis II Launch Day Updates 1014 upvotes, 888 comments
  26. DRAM pricing is killing the hobbyist SBC market 565 upvotes, 497 comments
  27. Show HN: I built a DNS resolver from scratch in Rust – no DNS libraries 43 upvotes, 33 comments
  28. Reinventing the Pull Request 49 upvotes, 41 comments
  29. On the trail of ancient art, deep in the Sahara 5 upvotes, 1 comments
  30. Telli (YC F24) is hiring engineers, designers, and more (on-site, Berlin) 0 upvotes, 0 comments
